summary: Close the M2 native project-snapshot path-aliasing finding by rejecting every child component that cannot be represented exactly as Unicode before directory traversal or file reads, instead of substituting replacement characters in portable file keys.
specSections: Section 2.7 test-first evidence and milestone gates
M2 C6 folder-backed desktop project storage

decisions.decision: Require every native project child component to convert exactly to Unicode before inspecting metadata, descending into a directory, or reading file contents. If any component is unsupported, reject the complete snapshot with a stable error; never construct a portable key with replacement characters and never return a partial file envelope.
decisions.rationale: The application boundary represents project-relative paths as Unicode strings with slash separators. Lossy conversion can map distinct native names to the same string, causing file-key collisions or later operations to address a different file. Failing closed preserves the one-root atomic snapshot guarantee established by D-0192/D-0193 while valid Unicode names retain their existing portable representation.

testEvidence.criterion: Unsupported child components are rejected instead of replaced
testEvidence.test: Construct a pure relative path containing an ill-formed UTF-16 child component on Windows, and the corresponding non-UTF-8 byte component on Unix, then require exact rejection. Also retain a Unix filesystem regression that creates a valid file plus a non-UTF-8 child and requires the complete snapshot result to be Err rather than a partial envelope.

red.outcome: ASSERTION-LEVEL RED (execution recorded immediately afterward; original wall-clock instant was not separately retained): test result: FAILED. 0 passed; 1 failed; 0 ignored; 0 measured; 27 filtered out; finished in 0.00s. Failure at src/project_storage.rs:644:9: assertion `left == right` failed; left: Ok("parts/�/wheel.scad") (the command printed the literal U+FFFD replacement character); right: Err("Project entry path contains a file or folder name that is not valid Unicode."). No warnings were emitted.

cargo test --locked --manifest-path src/desktop-shell/src-tauri/Cargo.toml rejects_non_unicode_child_components_without_lossy_substitution -- --nocapture
cargo test --locked --manifest-path src/desktop-shell/src-tauri/Cargo.toml project_storage::tests -- --nocapture

The binary target separately reported 0 passed; 0 failed; 0 ignored; 0 measured; 0 filtered out; finished in 0.00s. No failures or warnings were emitted. The Windows run exercised the ill-formed UTF-16 pure conversion; the cfg(unix) real-filesystem test is retained for Unix execution and was not claimed as executed on this Windows host.

testEvidence.criterion: Valid paths and Rust static gates remain clean
testEvidence.test: Keep valid nested Unicode child conversion and the existing text, binary, canonical-root, mutation, and escape tests green, then run formatting, all-target check, and warning-denying Clippy on the desktop crate.

red.outcome: The same assertion-level RED above established that the widened static run was necessary: one unsupported component still produced Ok("parts/�/wheel.scad") (literal U+FFFD) rather than the required error.

green.command:
cargo fmt --manifest-path src/desktop-shell/src-tauri/Cargo.toml -- --check
cargo check --locked --manifest-path src/desktop-shell/src-tauri/Cargo.toml --all-targets
cargo clippy --locked --manifest-path src/desktop-shell/src-tauri/Cargo.toml --all-targets -- -D warnings
cargo test --locked --manifest-path src/desktop-shell/src-tauri/Cargo.toml -- --nocapture
pnpm.cmd lint
pnpm.cmd check:provenance
green.outcome: GREEN: rustfmt emitted no diff or diagnostics; cargo check finished the dev profile in 55.09s with no warnings; Clippy finished the dev profile in 5.47s with -D warnings and no warnings. The complete desktop Rust library reported `test result: ok. 28 passed; 0 failed; 0 ignored; 0 measured; 0 filtered out; finished in 0.06s`; the binary and doctest targets each separately reported `test result: ok. 0 passed; 0 failed; 0 ignored; 0 measured; 0 filtered out; finished in 0.00s`. Biome reported `Checked 301 files in 297ms. No fixes applied.` and source policy passed. The provenance command reported `Provenance ledger passed validation (baseline mode; no PR base supplied).` No failures or warnings were emitted.

questions: This correction extends the exact non-Unicode project-root rejection already recorded by D-0193 to every child file and folder component; it does not change supported Unicode path behavior.
No owner similarity-gate content, comparison repository, workflow file, M3 work, installer work, or prohibited source was opened, executed, or modified.
attestation: No prohibited source was consulted in producing this change.
